name: Doc AST: Inline Expression Evaluation & Macro Unification

content.description:
# Doc AST: Inline Expression Evaluation & Macro Unification

**Status:** Proposal

The `:doc` AST currently has two kinds of inline nodes that fetch data at render time: `:field-ref` and `:transclusion`. Both are implemented as hardcoded renderer special cases rather than as Hob expressions. This proposal replaces them with standard Hob code, enabled by adding inline expression evaluation to the doc renderer.

See [Hob Language Design Direction](item://c0c0c0c0-0058-0000-0000-000000000000) for overall principles. See [Transparency & Conceptual Uniformity](item://c0c0c0c0-0093-0000-0000-000000000000) for the `:doc` AST spec.

## Current State

### The two expression-like inline nodes

| Node | AST | Renderer does |
|------|-----|---------------|
| `:field-ref` | `[:field-ref {s: "weight"}]` | `api.getField(currentItemId, "weight")` |
| `:transclusion` | `[:transclusion {s: itemId} {s: "description"}]` | `api.getField(itemId, "description")` |

Both call `get-field` and render the result. The only difference is the target item.

### The dispatch boundary

The renderer separates structure from computation by tag prefix:
- **`:keyword`** tags → doc structure, renderer handles directly
- **Bare symbol** tags → Hob expression, evaluator handles

This dispatch exists in `renderNode` (block level) but NOT in `renderInlineNode`. Hob expressions can only appear at block level today.

## Proposal

### 1. Add inline expression evaluation

Add evaluator fallback to `renderInlineNode`, matching what `renderNode` already does at block level:

```javascript
// In renderInlineNode, after the switch statement:
default: {
  if (!tag.startsWith(':')) {
    const result = await api.evaluateHob(node, { item });
    if (result != null) await renderInlineNode(result, parent, api, item);
  }
}
```

This is the key enabler. Once inline expressions work, any Hob expression — including macro calls — can appear anywhere in a document.

### 2. Replace `:transclusion` with `get-field` calls

The `:transclusion` node type is removed from the renderer. The stored AST changes from keyword to bare symbol:

```
;; Before (renderer special case)
[:transclusion {s: "item-id"} {s: "description"} {s: "display"}]

;; After (standard Hob expression)
["get-field", "@item-id", ":description"]
```

The `@@` autocomplete in field-view-doc-editable continues to work — it just inserts a `get-field` call instead of a `:transclusion` node.

### 3. Replace `:field-ref` with `get-field :self`

Same treatment:

```
;; Before
[:field-ref {s: "weight"}]

;; After
["get-field", ":self", ":weight"]
```

The `{{` autocomplete inserts `(get-field :self :fieldName)` instead of a `:field-ref` node.

### 4. `transclude` function with chrome/attribution

`transclude` is a **function** (not a macro) that calls `get-field` AND wraps the result with source attribution metadata:

```hob
(transclude @id :description)                 ;; default chrome
(transclude @id :description :chrome :subtle) ;; small attribution link
(transclude @id :description :chrome :none)   ;; suppress chrome
```

It returns a wrapper value (e.g. `{_hobType: "transclusion", value, sourceId, sourceField, chrome}`) that the renderer detects and turns into attribution UI — a collapsible container with source link, matching the existing markdown transclusion chrome.

This is meaningfully different from bare `get-field`:
- `get-field` — data fetch, no attribution
- `transclude` — data fetch + attribution chrome

The `@@` autocomplete inserts `transclude` calls (with chrome). The `{{` autocomplete inserts `(get-field :self :fieldName)` directly — local field refs don't need attribution since they reference the current item's own data.

## What this unifies

After this change, the doc AST has a clean two-category split:

| Category | Examples | Who handles |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| **Structure** | `:p`, `:h1`, `:strong`, `:em`, `:code`, `:link`, `:item-ref`, `:hr`, `:code-block`, `:ul`/`:ol`/`:li`, `:blockquote` | Renderer directly |
| **Expressions** | `get-field`, `let`, `map`, `if`, any user-defined macro | Hob evaluator |

`:item-ref` stays as a renderer node because it's pure structure (a clickable link) — it doesn't fetch or compute anything.

## Markdown transclusion features

Markdown transclusion ([Transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0035-0000-0000-000000000000)) supports features beyond simple field access. With inline expression evaluation, these become composable Hob expressions rather than URL query parameters:

| Markdown syntax | Hob expression |
|----------------|----------------|
| `![](item://id)` | `(transclude @id :description)` — defaults to `:description` |
| `![](item://id#field)` | `(transclude @id :field)` — field with chrome |
| `![](item://id#code?region=name)` | `(transclude @id :code :region "name")` |
| `![](item://id#code?lines=10-20)` | `(transclude @id :code :lines 10 20)` |
| `![](item://id?symbol=name)` | `(transclude @id :code :symbol "name")` |
| `![](item://id?call=fn)` | `(call-export @id "fn")` |

The `transclude` function handles region/lines/symbol extraction internally, keeping the call site clean. For cases where you want the raw data without chrome, use `get-field` and the extraction helpers directly:

```hob
(extract-region (get-field @id :code) "name")
(extract-lines (get-field @id :code) 10 20)
(extract-symbol @id "name")
```

The helper functions (`extract-region`, `extract-lines`, `extract-symbol`, `call-export`) would be stdlib additions. Each is independently useful beyond transclusion.

## Migration

Existing `:transclusion` and `:field-ref` nodes in stored ASTs need manual conversion. Since `:doc` AST adoption is early, this is a small number of items. The markdown-to-doc-ast converter (`d0d0d0d0-0008`) would be updated to emit `get-field` calls instead of `:transclusion`/`:field-ref` nodes.

## Editable view changes

The editable view (field-view-doc-editable) currently renders `:transclusion` and `:field-ref` as non-editable inline chips. After this change:

- Chips render for any non-keyword inline expression (not just specific node types)
- The chip display text can be derived from the expression structure (e.g., show item name for `get-field @id ...`)
- DOM-to-AST sync reconstructs the expression from chip `data-*` attributes
- The `@@` and `{{` autocomplete flows produce expression nodes instead of keyword nodes

## Design decisions

### Provenance is universal

The renderer wraps ALL inline expression results in a `<span>` with `data-provenance-*` attributes — not just transclusions. This is consistent with Hobson's principle that everything should be inspectable. The provenance span records the expression that produced the value, enabling debugging and tooling.

### Inline errors follow block approach

The block-level evaluator fallback already renders errors in a styled div. Inline expression errors follow the same pattern: a styled `<span>` with error message and the source expression, using `color: var(--color-danger)`. No special-casing per expression type.

### Chrome/attribution via `transclude` function

`transclude` is a function (not a macro) that wraps `get-field` results with source metadata. The renderer detects `transclusion` wrapper values and generates attribution chrome — matching the existing markdown transclusion UI (collapsible `<details>` with source link and navigation).

Chrome levels: `:full` (collapsible container with header), `:subtle` (small link marker), `:none` (no wrapper). Default TBD based on context — block-level transclusions probably want `:full`, inline probably `:subtle`.

Bare `get-field` calls produce no chrome. This gives users explicit control: `transclude` when you want attribution, `get-field` when you want raw data.
